_description: Simulation parameters for numerical stability and accuracy

_sizing_notes.timestep_selection: dt must be small enough for numerical stability but large enough for efficient simulation. Rule of thumb: dt < τ_min / 5, where τ_min is the smallest thermal time constant in the system.
_sizing_notes.chip_level: Chip thermal nodes have small capacitance (C ~ 8-200 J/K) and moderate resistance (R ~ 0.5-2 K/W), giving τ ~ 4-400s. Safe dt: 0.5-2.0s.
_sizing_notes.rack_level: Rack loops have larger thermal mass. Safe dt: 1.0-5.0s.
_sizing_notes.datacenter_level: CDU and cooling tower have large thermal masses. Safe dt: 5.0-30.0s.
_sizing_notes.rk4_substeps: CompOpt uses RK4 integration with automatic sub-stepping. The code computes max_stable_dt and subdivides if needed.

fast_training._description: Aggressive timesteps for faster RL training (may reduce accuracy)

high_fidelity._description: Conservative timesteps for high accuracy (slower simulation)
